steps.title: Spot the key tension
steps.content:
The word “however” signals a direct contrast: Marks & Costa found no enduring investment increases from liberalization (studying 1976-1993), while Harris found significant increases in the three years immediately after liberalization.

The phrase “Taken together” requires reconciling these seemingly opposed findings.

steps.title: Reconcile both findings
steps.content: D directly resolves the tension: short-term investment gains (as Harris found) that don't endure (as Marks & Costa found). The other choices each fail to capture this dual pattern. This confirms D is correct.
